response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with broad language exposure and solid craftsmanship practices, but falls well short of the core minimum qualifications. The role requires 8 years of C++ programming; her C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with most of her career in TypeScript/Node.js, D, and web development. Large-scale infrastructure exposure exists (low-latency D services at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second, Kubernetes deployments), but it is well under the 5-year bar, and there is no formal technical-leadership or cloud-platform ownership experience matching the tech-lead mandate.
strengths: High-throughput, low-latency systems experience on a micro-services platform handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus active mentoring via workshops, dojos, and courses
Broad, adaptable technical range across backend, infrastructure tooling, DevOps setup, and open-source project ownership
concerns: Far below the required 8 years of C++ experience (~1 year total, none since 2015)
No formal technical-lead role leading strategy, cross-team alignment, or managing project priorities and deadlines
Limited production cloud-architecture and compute-platform experience; background is primarily product/web engineering, not infrastructure platforms
